TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit DisplayNameInfo extends System.Object

Docs.summary: Resolves the display name of a validated member (property, parameter, or type). Each , , and may carry a single instance that encapsulates the strategy for producing its display name at validation time.

remarks.para: Implementations encode a single source of the display name (for example, a literal value from , a static resource accessor for [Display(ResourceType = ..., Name = ...)], or a custom strategy). The validation pipeline calls once per member validation and uses the result, falling back to the CLR member name when the implementation returns .

Implementations may participate in localization by inspecting on . Implementations that source their value from a static resource (the path) typically bypass because the resource lookup is the canonical source for the localized name.

MemberSignature.Value: .method familyhidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed
public abstract string? GetDisplayName (Microsoft.Extensions.Validation.ValidateContext context, string memberName, Type? type);
.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance string GetDisplayName(class Microsoft.Extensions.Validation.ValidateContext context, string memberName, class System.Type type) cil managed
Public MustOverride Function GetDisplayName (context As ValidateContext, memberName As String, type As Type) As String
abstract member GetDisplayName : Microsoft.Extensions.Validation.ValidateContext * string * Type -> string
public: abstract System::String ^ GetDisplayName(Microsoft::Extensions::Validation::ValidateContext ^ context, System::String ^ memberName, Type ^ type);

Docs.param: The current validation context. Provides access to for implementations that delegate to .
The CLR member name (property name, parameter name, or type name) being validated. Implementations may use this as a fallback display name or as a localization lookup key.
The type that declares the member for property-level validation, the validated type itself for type-level validation, or for parameter validation.
Docs.summary: Resolves the display name to use when reporting validation errors for the member.
Docs.returns: The display name for the member, or when no value can be produced. The validation pipeline falls back to in the case.
